title: PublicationRegistryManifest
description:
Represents the publication manifest for a packaged semantic convention registry.

This is produced by `weaver registry package` and describes the contents of
a self-contained registry artifact, including its resolved schema location.

description.description: An optional description of the registry.

file_format.description:
The file format version of this publication manifest.
Always `"manifest/2.0.0"`in this version.

resolved_schema_uri.description: URI pointing to the resolved telemetry schema included in this package.

schema_url.description:
The schema URL for this registry.
Uniquely identifies the registry and its version.

Dependency.description: Represents a dependency of a semantic convention registry.

registry_path.description:
The path to the dependency (optional).
This can be either:
- A manifest of a published registry
- A directory containing the raw definition.

schema_url.description:
The schema URL for the dependency (required).
It must follow OTel schema URL format, which is: `http[s]://server[:port]/path/<version>`.
This is not necessarily the URL registry can be accessed at, but it provides
a unique identifier for the dependency registry and its version.

When registry is not published yet, this field should be populated with a placeholder URL,
but it must follow the URL format and include a version segment.
The actual registry files can be provided in `registry_path` field.

SchemaUrl.description:
Represents the schema URL of a registry, which serves as a unique identifier for the registry
along with its version.

Stability.description: The level of stability for a definition. Defined in [OTEP-232](https://github.com/open-telemetry/oteps/blob/main/text/0232-maturity-of-otel.md)

oneOf.description: A definition in development. Formally known as experimental.

VirtualDirectoryPath.description:
Represents a virtual path pointing to a directory-like resource.

Supported formats include:
- **Local directories** (`/path/to/directory`)
- **Local archives** (`/path/to/archive.zip` or `/path/to/archive.tar.gz`)
- **Remote archives** (`https://example.com/archive.zip` or `.tar.gz`)
- **Git repositories** (`https://github.com/user/repo.git`)

Paths may optionally specify:
- A sub-folder within the archive or repository via `[sub_folder]`
- [Not Yet Implemented] A specific Git refspec (branch, tag, or commit) via `@refspec`
